test_headline.threshold_selection_rule: argmax F1 on VAL 2019, frozen and applied to TEST 2020 (test labels never used in threshold selection)

classifier_population.definition: Full year-stratified 2014-2020 panel; rows with a risk_label and contract_value_log, temporally split into TRAIN/VAL/TEST. This is the population the classifier is fit and evaluated on.
classifier_population.note: label_positives_total reconciles to the binary class-1 count (class_balance['1']).

rgov_scored_population.definition: Contracts that receive an HONEST ensemble risk score (R_gov): TRAIN rows scored out-of-fold + TEST rows scored held-out. VAL 2019 is EXCLUDED because it is used only to select the operating threshold and is never scored.
rgov_scored_population.filter: award_year in TRAIN_YEARS (out-of-fold) UNION award_year in TEST_YEARS (held-out); VAL 2019 excluded
rgov_scored_population.why_differs_from_classifier_population: Excludes the 120,205 VAL-2019 rows (threshold-selection only, never scored): 714,681 panel - 120,205 VAL = 594,476 scored.

risk_buckets.reconciliation_note: n_high_risk + n_low_risk = 539,614 EXCLUDES the 54,862 MEDIUM rows and the 120,205 VAL rows; it is NOT the classifier population (714,681) and not the scored population (594,476).

enrichment_population.definition: high_risk_amendment_rate, low_risk_amendment_rate and enrichment_ratio are computed over the HIGH and LOW R_gov SCORE buckets of the rgov_scored_population (NOT the classifier label).

enrichment_population: rgov_scored_population HIGH and LOW R_gov score buckets (NOT the classifier label)

single_bidder_definitions.note: All rates are 'single-bidder among OBSERVED lots' (bid_count <= 1 where bid_count is non-null); they differ only by POPULATION and denominator, not definition. bid_count (lot_bidscount) coverage is sparse (~1.6% raw / ~3.6% scored panel), so the choice of denominator matters.

rq2_scored_observed.definition: single-bidder (bid_count <= 1) among rgov_scored_population contracts with non-null bid_count -- the value currently reported as single_bid_rate.
rq2_scored_observed.population: rgov_scored_population (TRAIN out-of-fold + TEST held-out; VAL excluded)

panel_observed.definition: single-bidder (bid_count <= 1) among full 2014-2020 panel contracts with non-null bid_count.

raw_dataset_observed_eda.definition: single-bidder rate among OBSERVED lots (lot_bidscount non-null) over the FULL raw ANAC dataset -- the 29.8% figure cited in the thesis.
raw_dataset_observed_eda.population: full raw IT_DIB_2023 dataset (~12.1M lot-level rows)
raw_dataset_observed_eda.source: docs/THRESHOLD_JUSTIFICATION.md Section 6 (bid-count field correction); an EDA-stage figure, NOT recomputed in the RQ2 run.

single_bidder_definitions.recommendation: Cite ONE definition consistently. The thesis 29.8% is the full-raw-dataset observed-lot rate; the RQ2 module reports the scored-panel observed-lot rate. They agree in DEFINITION and differ only in POPULATION/denominator.

rgov_single_bidder_corr_status.denominator: buyer-level rows of rgov_scored_population; single_bid_rate is defined only for buyers with at least one observed bid_count.
rgov_single_bidder_corr_status.exact_zero_mechanism: FIXED 2026-06-15. Previously single_bid_rate was null for buyers with no observed bid_count, the full-array np.std was NaN, the 'std > 0' guard was False, pearsonr was skipped and the function returned a fabricated 0.0. The metric now drops null pairs and reports an explicit 3-state result; at this coverage the state is 'degenerate_low_coverage' and the headline value is null.
rgov_single_bidder_corr_status.interpretation: Uninformative at this coverage (mirrors the RQ1 dependency_single_bidder_corr limitation, Section 9). The observed-only r is reported in 'observed_value' but is computed on an unrepresentative minority of buyers. Reported, not gated; relocated to rq2_feature_exploration_nulls.json.

auc_significance.method: DeLong paired AUC-difference test (fast closed-form, Sun & Xu 2014 / DeLong et al. 1988); no bootstrap, deterministic.
auc_significance.note: Substantiates or softens the 'XGB edges LR/RF' claim on the 2020 point estimate; reported as computed.

pr_auc_baseline_note: pr_auc_baseline is the 2020-test positive rate -- the PR-AUC of a random classifier. PR-AUC is base-rate-dependent and would be lower at the ~16% panel positive rate.
rf_cv_f1_note: shuffled-CV F1; base-rate-inflated and NOT comparable to temporal-test F1 (0.494) due to 2019->2020 COVID base-rate drift.
